response.justification: The candidate is a highly accomplished staff-level engineer with nearly two decades of experience, which makes him dramatically overqualified for an entry-level junior position and a significant retention and salary-expectation risk. Beyond the seniority mismatch, his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than Java (which appears only peripherally), and his basic German falls well short of the strong verbal and written German the client-facing insurance consulting work requires.
strengths: Completed computer science degree and deep, broad software engineering experience across web, backend, and embedded systems
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, agile practices, and active knowledge sharing through workshops and talks
Based in Germany (Berlin) with fluent English and demonstrated ability to work with cloud and containerized infrastructure
concerns: Severely overqualified for an entry-level role, posing high flight-risk and likely salary misalignment
Only basic German, while the role requires strong verbal and written German for client collaboration
Java is not his primary language; recent professional work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and agile fundamentals
